component_concepts: NAT Gateway vs. Instance
Role of Inbound and Outbound Rules in Security Groups and NACLs
Private vs. Public Subnet

questions.question: A company is designing a VPC architecture where instances in private subnets need to access the internet for software updates. Security is a top priority, so the design should minimize opened ports. Which combination of services and configurations will best meet these requirements?
questions.option1: Use a NAT Gateway for internet access and apply restrictive outbound rules in Security Groups for the private subnet instances.
questions.option2: Deploy a NAT Instance in a public subnet and use permissive inbound rules in NACLs for private subnets.
questions.option3: Place all instances in public subnets with private IPs and use a NAT Gateway for internet access.
questions.option4: Use an Internet Gateway attached directly to the private subnets with default NACL rules allowing all traffic.

component_concepts: Understanding CIDR Notation for Defining IP Ranges
Range of IPs Defined by Different Subnet Masks
Components of CIDR: Base IP and Subnet Mask

questions.question: In an AWS environment, how can CIDR notation be used to effectively define and allocate IP ranges for a VPC, and what impact does the subnet mask have on this allocation?
questions.option1: CIDR notation allows the specification of IP ranges with varying lengths of subnet masks, where a smaller subnet mask results in more available IP addresses in the range.
questions.option2: CIDR notation and subnet masks are used interchangeably, and the changes in subnet masks have no impact on the count of IP addresses available.
questions.option3: A larger subnet mask always results in a larger range of available IP addresses in CIDR allocation.
questions.option4: CIDR notation is used only to categorize different IP types like public and private, with no effect on IP range allocation.

component_concepts: Role of the Default VPC in AWS Accounts
Internet Connectivity in the Default VPC

questions.question: What is a key feature of the default VPC in AWS accounts regarding internet connectivity?
questions.option1: The default VPC is preconfigured to allow internet connectivity with instances having public IPs by default.
questions.option2: The default VPC does not permit any internet connectivity unless a NAT Gateway is manually set up.
questions.option3: The default VPC only allows internet connectivity through a VPN connection.
questions.option4: A default VPC is configured to only support private IP addresses, thus no internet access is possible.

component_concepts: Traffic Flow and Evaluation Process in Security Groups and NACLs
Impact of NACL Rules on Network Traffic
Statefulness in Security Groups

questions.question: A company is configuring their AWS VPC network security. They want to understand how the traffic flow is evaluated between security groups and NACLs, especially in terms of statefulness. Which of the following configurations best describes how traffic should be managed?
questions.option1: Security groups are stateful, meaning changes in one direction automatically apply to return traffic, whereas NACLs are stateless, requiring explicit rules for both inbound and outbound traffic.
questions.option2: Security groups are stateless, which means you must define rules for both inbound and outbound traffic, while NACLs automatically allow return traffic.
questions.option3: Both security groups and NACLs are stateful, allowing automatic return traffic in both directions without additional rules.
questions.option4: NACLs are stateful, whereas security groups are stateless, requiring rules for each direction separately.

component_concepts: Default NACL Behavior
Difference Between Security Groups and NACLs

questions.question: While setting up a new VPC, a network engineer needs to restrict traffic between subnets and configure security rules for instances. How does the default behavior of Network ACLs (NACLs) differ from Security Groups in this setup?
questions.option1: Default NACLs allow all inbound and outbound traffic, while Security Groups by default deny all traffic.
questions.option2: Default NACLs deny all inbound and outbound traffic, while Security Groups by default allow all traffic.
questions.option3: Default NACLs and Security Groups both deny all inbound and outbound traffic by default.
questions.option4: Default NACLs deny inbound traffic but allow outbound traffic, while Security Groups deny all outbound traffic.

component_concepts: Importance of CIDR in Network Security and Management
Using CIDR for Efficient IP Allocation in Networks
Using CIDR for Security Group Rules and Networking in AWS

questions.question: Your organization is designing a new VPC and is focused on efficient IP management and strong network security. How can CIDR be used to achieve both of these goals in AWS?
questions.option1: CIDR allows for precise IP address allocations, mitigating IP address wastage, and defining security group rules efficiently by grouping IPs into manageable ranges.
questions.option2: CIDR is mainly used for defining the volume of internet traffic allowed into a VPC, rather than managing internal networks or security.
questions.option3: CIDR in AWS subnets primarily enhances instances' performance by increasing available bandwidth.
questions.option4: CIDR automatically allocates more public IP addresses to increase external accessibility without a need for additional configurations.

component_concepts: Differences Between Public and Private IP Addresses in AWS
Understanding Route Tables and Their Role in Traffic Routing
Instance-Level Security with Security Groups

questions.question: A company is setting up an AWS VPC and needs to understand how public and private IP addresses are managed. They want to ensure the traffic routing is efficient while maintaining strict instance-level security. Which AWS features should they focus on to achieve this?
questions.option1: Assign public IPs to instances in public subnets and use Security Groups to control inbound and outbound traffic, while configuring Route Tables to manage traffic routing effectively.
questions.option2: Use private IPs for all instances and depend only on Network ACLs for instance-level security, while Route Tables are set to default.
questions.option3: Configure all instances with public IPs for better reachability and manage routing using a single global Route Table.
questions.option4: Rely solely on Security Groups and disregard the differences between public and private IPs, focusing instead on NAT Gateways for routing.

component_concepts: Private IP vs Public IP
Internet Gateway and Its Role in Providing Internet Access
Role of NAT Gateway and Internet Gateway in Network Traffic

questions.question: An e-commerce company is deploying a web application on AWS. The application servers are in a private subnet, and the front-end servers are in a public subnet. What configuration should they use to allow front-end servers to access the internet and ensure application servers can download updates?
questions.option1: Use an Internet Gateway for front-end servers' internet access and a NAT Gateway for application servers' updates.
questions.option2: Assign public IPs to all servers and use an Internet Gateway for internet access.
questions.option3: Use a NAT Gateway for both front-end and application servers to access the internet.
questions.option4: Configure a VPC Peering connection between subnets for internet access.

component_concepts: Difference Between Public and Private IP Addresses
Managing Route Tables for Network Security
Subnet-Level Security with NACLs

questions.question: A company is designing its VPC and wants to ensure their private subnet can securely communicate with a database hosted in the public subnet while maintaining strict network security. What steps should they take regarding IP management and route tables?
questions.option1: Use private IP addresses in the private subnet, configure route tables to deny public internet traffic, and apply a NACL to allow database communication.
questions.option2: Assign public IP addresses to instances in both subnets and use default NACL settings to allow open communication.
questions.option3: Use private IP addresses for the database and configure the route table to allow all traffic from the internet for easy access.
questions.option4: Assign Elastic IPs to all instances for better management and apply NACLs to restrict database traffic from the private subnet.

component_concepts: DNS and Route Table Configuration for VPC Endpoints
IPv4 CIDR Block and its Significance

questions.question: An IT company wishes to access an AWS service over a private connection using VPC endpoints. How should they configure their VPC to achieve this while considering IP addressing?
questions.option1: Configure the route table to point traffic destined for the service to the VPC endpoint and ensure the VPC has a valid IPv4 CIDR block assigned.
questions.option2: Use an Internet Gateway for the route table and use any IPv6 CIDR block for addressing.
questions.option3: Set up a VPN connection for AWS service access and implement PrivateLink without any specific IPv4 address considerations.
questions.option4: Assign random IP addresses to the VPC and set up a NAT Gateway for directing service traffic.

component_concepts: Network ACLs and Their Default Rules
Priority and Precedence of NACL Rules

questions.question: An organization is setting up Network ACLs for their VPC to control access to subnet resources. Which consideration is essential when configuring the rules to ensure proper traffic filtering?
questions.option1: The rules with the lowest number have the highest priority and are evaluated first.
questions.option2: Network ACL rules are evaluated based on the order in which they are added.
questions.option3: Network ACLs allow only inbound traffic by default and require explicit outbound rules.
questions.option4: Network ACLs are stateful and automatically allow return traffic without additional rules.

component_concepts: Impact of Subnet Mask on the Number of Available IP Addresses
Applications of Different Private IP Ranges

questions.question: An AWS network architect needs to create a subnet that supports up to 500 IP addresses for internal applications. Which subnet CIDR range should they select to optimize IP utilization while conforming to AWS best practices?
questions.option1: Choose a /23 subnet mask as it provides 512 IP addresses, including AWS reserved IPs, accommodating 500 usable addresses.
questions.option2: Select a /22 subnet mask since it provides just enough IPs for 500 devices.
questions.option3: Use a /24 subnet mask because it suitably matches the requirement of 500 IP addresses.
questions.option4: Implement a /20 subnet mask to ensure maximum growth potential and IP availability.

component_concepts: Automatic Return Traffic in Stateful Security Groups

questions.question: In AWS, how does a stateful security group handle return traffic for existing outbound connections?
questions.option1: The stateful security group automatically allows return traffic for outbound connections without an explicit inbound rule.
questions.option2: Return traffic must be explicitly allowed by adding inbound rules for every outbound connection.
questions.option3: Stateful security groups require a dedicated NAT Gateway to handle return traffic.
questions.option4: Return traffic must be handled by setting up a VPC Peering connection between the source and destination.

component_concepts: Using Flow Logs to Monitor and Troubleshoot Connectivity Issues
Identifying Problematic IPs and Ports from Flow Logs

questions.question: A company notices intermittent connectivity issues within their VPC. They have set up VPC Flow Logs to diagnose the problem. What should they specifically look for in the flow logs to identify the source of the issue?
questions.option1: Review the logs to identify any rejected traffic and the associated source and destination IPs and ports.
questions.option2: Check the flow logs for latency issues by reviewing the timestamp of accepted connections.
questions.option3: Examine successful DNS resolution incidents within the logs.
questions.option4: Look for instances of Elastic Load Balancing errors in the flow logs.

component_concepts: NAT Gateway with High Availability
Subnet Allocation and Availability Zones
Networking Costs in AWS

questions.question: A company is designing a multi-AZ architecture in AWS to ensure high availability and cost management for their web applications. They plan to deploy a NAT Gateway for internet access from private subnets and use multiple availability zones for their setup. What considerations should they keep in mind to balance their networking costs and availability?
questions.option1: Deploy a NAT Gateway in each availability zone to ensure high availability and minimize data transfer costs between zones.
questions.option2: Deploy a single NAT Gateway in one availability zone to save on deployment costs, relying on cross-AZ traffic when needed.
questions.option3: Use only private IPv6 addresses to reduce data transfer costs and remove the need for a NAT Gateway.
questions.option4: Leverage VPC Peering to connect different zones and use a single NAT Gateway for the entire VPC.

component_concepts: AWS Network Firewall Use Cases
NAT Instance Use Case

questions.question: A financial services company needs to secure and control traffic flow between their on-premise environments and AWS. They require logging for traffic and protection against common security threats, while also allowing certain instances that don't have public IP addresses to access the internet for patches and updates. What solution should they implement?
questions.option1: Deploy AWS Network Firewall for traffic filtering and monitoring along with NAT Instances for internet access from private instances.
questions.option2: Use AWS WAF for logging and security and assign elastic IPs to each private instance for internet connectivity.
questions.option3: Implement Security Groups for traffic filtering and CloudFront with Lambda@Edge for internet access.
questions.option4: Set up Direct Connect for secure communication and VPN for internet access from private instances.

component_concepts: VPC Peering Use Case
Bastion Host Use Case

questions.question: Your company has two VPCs in different regions that need to be configured for secure external access during scheduled maintenance on private EC2 instances. How should you configure the architecture using a bastion host?
questions.option1: Configure VPC peering between the VPCs and use a bastion host to provide secure SSH access to instances in both VPCs.
questions.option2: Set up a NAT Gateway in each VPC to allow public access and manage security through security groups alone.
questions.option3: Use AWS Direct Connect to establish a private connection between the VPCs and eliminate the need for a bastion host.
questions.option4: Deploy an internet gateway in each VPC for direct SSH access to the instances without a bastion host.

component_concepts: Flow Logs and Their Uses
Inter-Region Traffic
Traffic Types and Costs

questions.question: A company is managing multiple AWS regions and wants to analyze cross-region traffic patterns and associated costs. They are considering enabling flow logs to capture traffic data, and want to understand the cost implications of different traffic types. Which approach should the company take to effectively achieve this goal using AWS services?
questions.option1: Enable VPC Flow Logs for each VPC in every region to capture all traffic, then use Amazon Athena to analyze the logs and understand different traffic costs.
questions.option2: Rely on CloudWatch Metrics to get complete visibility into inter-region traffic and directly calculate costs without enabling flow logs.
questions.option3: Set up Direct Connect for all regions and use AWS Cost Explorer to analyze traffic patterns without needing flow log data.
questions.option4: Use AWS Trusted Advisor to get insights into inter-region traffic and possible cost savings without enabling additional services.

component_concepts: Free Access to Amazon S3 and DynamoDB Using Gateway Endpoints
Accessing AWS Services Privately Using VPC Endpoints
NAT Gateway vs VPC Endpoint

questions.question: An organization wants to improve security and reduce data transfer costs by enabling private access to Amazon S3 from their VPC. Which approach should they adopt?
questions.option1: Use a Gateway Endpoint to access Amazon S3 within the VPC without using public IPs.
questions.option2: Configure a NAT Gateway to route traffic to Amazon S3 to keep it secure.
questions.option3: Deploy a VPC Endpoint Interface for Amazon S3 to facilitate private access.
questions.option4: Use a public VPC Endpoint to access Amazon S3 with enhanced security.

component_concepts: Cross-Region and Cross-Account Connectivity Using Transit Gateway
Sending Flow Logs to Different AWS Services
Capturing Information from IP Traffic Using VPC Flow Logs

questions.question: An organization operates in multiple AWS regions and accounts, requiring seamless network connectivity across them. They also need to monitor and analyze network traffic for security purposes. What solution should they implement?
questions.option1: Deploy a Transit Gateway for cross-region and cross-account connectivity, and send VPC flow logs to Amazon CloudWatch for traffic analysis.
questions.option2: Use Direct Connect to establish cross-region network links, while Flow Logs are stored in S3 for subsequent analysis.
questions.option3: Set up VPN connections for each region and account, and configure Flow Logs to be analyzed directly using AWS CloudTrail.
questions.option4: Implement multiple VPC Peering connections, and aggregate VPC Flow Logs through AWS Lambda and store them in Glacier.

component_concepts: Public and Private IPv4 DNS Names for EC2 Instances
Auto-assigned Public IPv4 Addresses for Subnets

questions.question: A company is deploying an application on an EC2 instance within a VPC and wants to ensure that the instance is accessible via the internet through its DNS name. They prefer a dynamic IP addressing approach for easier management. Which configuration approach aligns with this requirement?
questions.option1: Ensure the subnet is public and set EC2 instances to automatically acquire public IP addresses, utilizing their public DNS names for internet accessibility.
questions.option2: Enable a VPN connection between the VPC and the corporate data center to provide public DNS names.
questions.option3: Allocate Elastic IPs for each instance and manually configure their DNS names to be public.
questions.option4: Assign private IPs to all instances and rely on an Application Load Balancer for internet access using private DNS names.

component_concepts: Use Case for NACLs in Blocking Specific IPs
Statelessness in NACLs

questions.question: Your organization needs to block inbound traffic from a specific IP address to enhance security. Which AWS feature should be leveraged to achieve this, and what important characteristic must be considered?
questions.option1: Use Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) for blocking the IP address, considering that NACLs are stateless and require rules for both inbound and outbound traffic.
questions.option2: Implement Security Groups to block the IP, understanding that Security Groups are stateful and track connection states automatically.
questions.option3: Leverage AWS Shield to block IP addresses at the network level, noting its cost implications.
questions.option4: Set up AWS WAF to block the IP at the edge, recognizing the potential delay from edge to VPC traffic.

component_concepts: Using CloudFront with S3
High Availability vs Cost Optimization

questions.question: A company is deploying a global web application and wants to ensure high availability and cost-efficient content delivery from their S3 bucket. Which solution best achieves this goal using AWS services?
questions.option1: Integrate Amazon CloudFront with the S3 bucket to cache content at edge locations, optimizing performance and availability while reducing data transfer costs.
questions.option2: Enable Cross-Region Replication for the S3 bucket to ensure high availability and directly serve requests from each region.
questions.option3: Utilize AWS Direct Connect to deliver content from the S3 bucket, ensuring a dedicated network connection for high availability.
questions.option4: Deploy the application using Amazon EC2 with Elastic Load Balancing to distribute traffic globally directly from the S3 bucket.

component_concepts: IPv6 for VPC
Implicit and Explicit Association of Route Tables with Subnets

questions.question: An organization is migrating their IPv4 VPC to support IPv6 while managing traffic efficiently. They need to ensure that specific subnets have custom route tables without manual associations disrupting traffic. What is an effective strategy to achieve this?
questions.option1: Enable IPv6 and use explicit association of route tables for subnets that require custom configurations.
questions.option2: Enable IPv6 and rely on implicit association as it automatically handles subnet routing without manual intervention.
questions.option3: Assign IPv6 CIDR blocks and use default route tables, as custom configurations are not supported with IPv6.
questions.option4: Disable IPv6 on subnets that require custom routes to simplify route table management.

component_concepts: S3 Data Transfer Pricing
Optimizing Costs with Private IPs
Cross-AZ Traffic

questions.question: A company is architecting its AWS environment to minimize costs associated with data transfer between services hosted in multiple availability zones. Which strategy should they employ to optimize costs when accessing S3 buckets from EC2 instances across AZs?
questions.option1: Utilize S3 VPC Endpoint to ensure data paths stay within the AWS backbone, minimizing inter-AZ data transfer costs.
questions.option2: Always use public IP addresses for S3 access to leverage AWS's global network infrastructure.
questions.option3: Enable cross-region replication on S3 buckets to ensure data is available locally in each AZ.
questions.option4: Route all S3 traffic through a public NAT Gateway to take advantage of data transfer discounts.

component_concepts: Levels of Flow Logs: VPC, Subnet, ENI
ENI as an Entry Point for Private AWS Services

questions.question: An enterprise hosts a private application inside a VPC. They want to monitor traffic at multiple granular levels and use Elastic Network Interfaces (ENI) for entry points to enhance security. Which approach should they take to ensure comprehensive logging and secure access?
questions.option1: Enable VPC, Subnet, and ENI level flow logs to capture traffic at different granularities, and configure ENIs for secure private access.
questions.option2: Use only VPC level flow logs for network traffic monitoring and configure classic load balancers as entry points.
questions.option3: Implement AWS Shield for traffic monitoring and use NAT Instances for entry points to the private services.
questions.option4: Use CloudFormation templates for setting up flow logs and configure ELB flow logs for entry point traffic monitoring.

component_concepts: Difference Between Interface Endpoints and Gateway Endpoints
Importance of Ephemeral Ports in Network Communication

questions.question: A company is setting up their AWS architecture and needs to connect their VPC to various AWS services like S3 and DynamoDB. They want to utilize VPC endpoints for this purpose. How should they decide between interface and gateway endpoints, and what role do ephemeral ports play in this setup?
questions.option1: Use interface endpoints for services like DynamoDB and consider the use of ephemeral ports for establishing transient client-server connections.
questions.option2: Use gateway endpoints exclusively, as they apply to all types of AWS services and manage ephemeral port requirements automatically.
questions.option3: Choose interface endpoints for S3 because they integrate with the network interface, and rely on static port allocations for network traffic.
questions.option4: Interface endpoints are used primarily for services that rely on HTTP/S protocols, and ephemeral ports are irrelevant in this context.

component_concepts: Accessing Both Public and Private AWS Resources via VIFs
Direct Connect for Real-Time Data Feeds and Hybrid Environments

questions.question: A company needs a dedicated network connection to AWS for real-time data feeds and access to both public and private resources. How can Virtual Interfaces (VIFs) be configured to achieve this with AWS Direct Connect?
questions.option1: Set up both a Public VIF and a Private VIF through AWS Direct Connect to enable access to public AWS services and private VPC resources concurrently.
questions.option2: Configure a single Private VIF to access both public and private AWS resources for real-time data processing needs.
questions.option3: Establish a Public VIF and use an Internet Gateway to reach private resources over AWS Direct Connect.
questions.option4: Leverage a Transit Gateway along with a default VIF to route traffic to both public and private AWS domains.

component_concepts: Integration of Direct Connect and VPN with Transit Gateway
Connecting Multiple VPCs Through Transit Gateway
Simplifying Network Topologies with Transit Gateway

questions.question: An organization plans to enhance its network architecture by connecting its on-premises data center to multiple AWS VPCs. They want to improve data transfer efficiency without compromising security. What is the best approach to achieve this using AWS services?
questions.option1: Integrate Direct Connect with a Transit Gateway to connect multiple VPCs and establish a secure VPN connection for backup and additional security.
questions.option2: Use only a VPN connection to link the on-premises data center directly to each VPC, forgoing the use of Direct Connect or Transit Gateway.
questions.option3: Set up separate Direct Connect connections for each VPC, ensuring independent high-speed access to each VPC.
questions.option4: Use internet gateways to connect on-premises data centers to VPCs and manage security with NACLs and Security Groups.

component_concepts: Setting Up Virtual Private Gateway for Direct Connect
Connecting On-Premises Data Centers to AWS Using Direct Connect
Direct Connect Cost Considerations

questions.question: A corporation plans to enhance its on-premises data center connectivity to AWS using Direct Connect. They aim to set up a secure and efficient network connection while minimizing costs. What steps should they take in setting up the architecture?
questions.option1: Configure a Virtual Private Gateway, use Direct Connect for a dedicated connection, and optimize costs through monitoring usage and choosing appropriate port speeds.
questions.option2: Set up a NAT Gateway and use an Internet Gateway via Direct Connect to reduce latency and enhance security.
questions.option3: Implement AWS VPN with a Virtual Private Gateway to leverage existing broadband connections for cost savings.
questions.option4: Use VPC Peering and Public IP addresses to establish private connections between on-premises data centers and AWS via Direct Connect.

component_concepts: Using AWS PrivateLink for Secure Network Connections
Preferred Use Cases for Gateway Endpoints vs. Interface Endpoints

questions.question: A company needs to securely connect to AWS services and their own on-premises network without routing traffic over the public internet. They also require cost-effective solutions for accessing AWS services in the same AWS region. What combination of AWS services should they use?
questions.option1: Use AWS PrivateLink for secure connections to on-premises environments and Gateway Endpoints for accessing AWS services within the same region.
questions.option2: Set up an Internet Gateway for secure connections and Interface Endpoints for accessing AWS services.
questions.option3: Utilize a VPC Peering for secure network links and Direct Connect for accessing AWS services.
questions.option4: Deploy Transit Gateway for all traffic and Internet Gateway for connecting with AWS services.

component_concepts: Advantages of Direct Connect: Increased Bandwidth, Lower Cost, Consistent Network Experience
Establishing Transitive Peering Connections
Cost and Scalability Considerations for VPC Endpoints

questions.question: A large enterprise is designing a network architecture for their AWS and on-premises data centers. The goal is to maintain high performance and cost-efficiency. They plan to use Direct Connect for increased bandwidth and lower cost. Which approach should they take to also enable network-wide communication between multiple VPCs in the most cost-effective manner?
questions.option1: Use Direct Connect for AWS connectivity, and implement VPC Peering with centralized Transit VPC for transitive peering connections.
questions.option2: Set up multiple VPC Endpoints for inter-VPC communication, leveraging Direct Connect for cost efficiency.
questions.option3: Use Direct Connect for linking on-premises to AWS and establish individual VPC Peering connections for each VPC.
questions.option4: Implement a multi-region setup using Direct Connect in each region and rely on Internet Gateways for cross-region networking.

component_concepts: VPC Traffic Mirroring Use Case
Analyzing Flow Log Data with Athena and CloudWatch Logs Insights

questions.question: A financial company needs to monitor and analyze network traffic patterns within their VPC for security and compliance. They want to trace all network packets at the packet level and also perform detailed analysis of flow log data. Which AWS services and solutions should they implement to achieve this?
questions.option1: Implement VPC Traffic Mirroring to capture and analyze network packets in real-time while using Athena and CloudWatch Logs Insights for analyzing flow log data.
questions.option2: Use an Egress Only Internet Gateway to monitor outbound traffic, along with Direct Connect to perform deep packet inspection.
questions.option3: Set up an Internet Gateway combined with RDS logs for monitoring traffic and analyzing flow data.
questions.option4: Utilize Transit Gateway Flow Logs for detailed analysis and rely on EC2 instances for manual packet capture.

component_concepts: Egress Only Internet Gateway Use Case
Role of IANA in Defining Private and Public IP Address Ranges

questions.question: A security-focused company runs applications on IPv6-enabled instances within an AWS VPC. They want these instances to access the internet only for software updates but wish to avoid incoming internet traffic. What AWS service is ideal for this use case, and how does understanding IP address allocations by IANA assist in planning their architecture?
questions.option1: Use an Egress Only Internet Gateway to enable outbound IPv6 traffic while preventing inbound access, and refer to IANA documents to ensure proper use of global unicast address space.
questions.option2: Deploy an Internet Gateway, as it controls both inbound and outbound traffic, and use IANA guidelines to assign private IP ranges.
questions.option3: Set up a NAT Gateway to manage outbound traffic and use IANA documentation to assign public IP ranges to private subnets.
questions.option4: Utilize a VPC Peering setup for secure internet connectivity and consult IANA for allocating multicast IP addresses.
